---
title: "Isolation by Distance – Alabama Raccoons"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(vcfR)
library(adegenet)
library(poppr)
library(geosphere)
library(vegan)
# library(spdep)   # ← comment this out for now
library(igraph)
library(scales)

set.seed(123)

vcfFile <- "/scratch/Raccoon_andrea/gabe_trial/data/vcf/Raccoon.filtered.vcf"
labFile <- "/scratch/Raccoon_andrea/gabe_trial/scripts/PopulationGenetics/labels_coords.csv"
out_dir <- "/scratch/Raccoon_andrea/gabe_trial/results/IBD"

dir.create(out_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
```

## Load labels
```{r load-labels, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
labs <- read.csv(labFile)

head(labs)
```

## Read VCF and convert to genlight
```{r genlight, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
vcf <- read.vcfR(vcfFile, verbose = FALSE)
gl <- vcfR2genlight(vcf)

vcf_ids <- indNames(gl)

# --- clean IDs (common hidden issue) ---
labs$sampleID <- trimws(as.character(labs$sampleID))
vcf_ids       <- trimws(as.character(vcf_ids))

# --- check duplicates in labels ---
dup_labs <- labs$sampleID[duplicated(labs$sampleID)]
if (length(dup_labs) > 0) {
  message("Duplicate sampleIDs in labels (showing up to 20): ",
          paste(head(unique(dup_labs), 20), collapse=", "))
}

# --- what's missing? ---
missing_in_labels <- setdiff(vcf_ids, labs$sampleID)
missing_in_vcf    <- setdiff(labs$sampleID, vcf_ids)

if (length(missing_in_labels) > 0) {
  message("VCF IDs missing in labels (showing up to 20): ",
          paste(head(missing_in_labels, 20), collapse=", "))
}
if (length(missing_in_vcf) > 0) {
  message("Label IDs missing in VCF (showing up to 20): ",
          paste(head(missing_in_vcf, 20), collapse=", "))
}

# --- subset to intersection so analysis can proceed ---
keep_ids <- intersect(vcf_ids, labs$sampleID)

if (length(keep_ids) < 10) stop("Too few overlapping samples between VCF and labels to run IBD.")

# subset genlight to overlapping IDs
gl <- gl[match(keep_ids, vcf_ids), ]
vcf_ids2 <- indNames(gl)

# reorder labels to match genlight sample order
labs2 <- labs[match(vcf_ids2, labs$sampleID), ]
stopifnot(all(labs2$sampleID == vcf_ids2))

# assign populations
pop(gl) <- as.factor(labs2$county)

```

## Genetic distance
```{r gendist, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
gen_dist <- bitwise.dist(gl)          # returns a dist object
attr(gen_dist, "Labels") <- labs2$sampleID  # ensure labels persist

# also keep a matrix version if you want
gen_dist_mat <- as.matrix(gen_dist)
```

## Geographic distance (km)
```{r geodist, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
coords <- as.matrix(labs2[, c("lon","lat")])

geo_dist_mat <- distm(coords, fun = distHaversine) / 1000
rownames(geo_dist_mat) <- labs2$sampleID
colnames(geo_dist_mat) <- labs2$sampleID

geo_dist <- as.dist(geo_dist_mat)
attr(geo_dist, "Labels") <- labs2$sampleID
```

## Mantel test
```{r mantel, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
mantel_raw <- mantel(as.dist(gen_dist), as.dist(geo_dist),
                     method = "pearson", permutations = 1000)

geo_log <- log10(geo_dist + 1)

mantel_log <- mantel(as.dist(gen_dist), as.dist(geo_log),
                     method = "pearson", permutations = 1000)

mantel_raw
mantel_log
```


## Plot IBD: between-county comparisons in main panel,
## with within-county genetic-distance density on the right
```{r plotIBD, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# pairwise values from matrices
upper_idx <- upper.tri(gen_dist_mat)

geo_vals <- geo_dist_mat[upper_idx]
gen_vals <- gen_dist_mat[upper_idx]

# identify within-county pairs directly from county labels
county_mat <- outer(as.character(labs2$county), as.character(labs2$county), FUN = "==")
within_vals <- county_mat[upper_idx]

# split into within-county vs between-county
geo_between <- geo_vals[!within_vals]
gen_between <- gen_vals[!within_vals]

geo_within <- geo_vals[within_vals]
gen_within <- gen_vals[within_vals]

# check that within-county geographic distances are zero (or essentially zero)
summary(geo_within)

# dynamic x-axis so points are not cut off
xmax <- max(geo_between, na.rm = TRUE)
xpad <- xmax * 0.03


layout(matrix(c(1, 2), nrow = 1), widths = c(4.8, 1.2))

# --- Main scatter: BETWEEN-county comparisons only ---
par(mar = c(5, 5, 2, 1))

plot(
  geo_between, gen_between,
  pch = 21,
  col = "darkorchid4",
  bg = alpha("darkorchid3", 0.45),
  cex = 1.0,
  xlab = "Geographic distance (km)",
  ylab = "Genetic distance",
  main = ""
)

# regression line on between-county comparisons
ibd_lm <- lm(gen_between ~ geo_between)
abline(ibd_lm, col = "black", lwd = 2)

# fix x-axis range so no points are clipped
usr <- par("usr")
rect(usr[1], usr[3], xmax + xpad, usr[4], col = NA, border = NA)
plot.new()

plot(
  geo_between, gen_between,
  pch = 21,
  col = "darkorchid4",
  bg = alpha("darkorchid3", 0.45),
  cex = 1.0,
  xlab = "Geographic distance (km)",
  ylab = "Genetic distance",
  xlim = c(0, xmax + xpad),
  ylim = range(c(gen_between, gen_within), na.rm = TRUE),
  main = ""
)
abline(ibd_lm, col = "black", lwd = 2)

# --- Right panel: within-county genetic-distance density ---
par(mar = c(5, 0.5, 2, 3))

dens <- density(gen_within, na.rm = TRUE)

plot(
  dens$y, dens$x,
  type = "l",
  lwd = 2,
  col = "darkorchid4",
  xlab = "",
  ylab = "",
  yaxt = "n",
  xaxt = "n",
  ylim = range(c(gen_between, gen_within), na.rm = TRUE),
  main = ""
)

polygon(
  c(0, dens$y, 0),
  c(min(dens$x), dens$x, max(dens$x)),
  col = alpha("darkorchid3", 0.35),
  border = NA
)
lines(dens$y, dens$x, lwd = 2, col = "darkorchid4")

mtext("Within-county\ngenetic distance", side = 4, line = 1.2, cex = 0.9)

```

## Save Outputs
```{r save, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Save Mantel text outputs (what you already do)
writeLines(capture.output(mantel_raw),
           con = file.path(out_dir, "mantel_raw.txt"))

writeLines(capture.output(mantel_log),
           con = file.path(out_dir, "mantel_log.txt"))

# NEW: save distance objects for re-use (river barrier, etc.)
saveRDS(gen_dist, file = "/scratch/Raccoon_andrea/gabe_trial/scripts/PopulationGenetics/gen_dist.rds")
saveRDS(geo_dist, file = "/scratch/Raccoon_andrea/gabe_trial/scripts/PopulationGenetics/geo_dist.rds")

# Optional: save matrices too (handy for debugging/plots)
saveRDS(gen_dist_mat, file = "/scratch/Raccoon_andrea/gabe_trial/scripts/PopulationGenetics/gen_dist_matrix.rds")
saveRDS(geo_dist_mat, file = "/scratch/Raccoon_andrea/gabe_trial/scripts/PopulationGenetics/geo_dist_matrix.rds")
```

## Mantel correlogram test
```{r mantelcorr, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
## Mantel correlogram
library(vegan)

# choose distance class width (25–50 km works well for county data)
correlog <- mantel.correlog(
  D.eco = gen_dist_mat,
  D.geo = geo_dist_mat,
  n.class = 10,
  nperm = 1000
)

correlog
```

## Plot Mantel correlogram test
```{r Plotmantelcorr, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}

# Keep only bins with non-missing Mantel correlations
keep <- !is.na(correlog$mantel.res[, "Mantel.cor"])

mantel_res_plot <- correlog$mantel.res[keep, ]

# Midpoints for the bins that actually returned values
class_midpoints <- mantel_res_plot[, "class.index"]
mantel_r <- mantel_res_plot[, "Mantel.cor"]

# Labels for the bins that actually have results
class_labels <- c("25", "75", "125", "175", "225")

plot(
  class_midpoints, mantel_r,
  type = "b",
  pch = 21,
  bg = "darkorchid3",
  col = "black",
  xaxt = "n",
  xlab = "Geographic distance class (km)",
  ylab = "Mantel correlation (r)",
  xlim = c(0, 225)
)

axis(1, at = class_midpoints, labels = class_labels)

abline(h = 0, col = "#D3D3D3", lty = 2)

```
